conflicts.description: The prior sheet compresses the novel to about 6-8 weeks plus a short gap, but the manuscript still has the Blackbell gala in October and places Chapter 17 in the third week of March.
conflicts.canonical_value: Chapters 1-16 play out over several autumn weeks through the summit, and Chapter 17 jumps ahead to the third week of March.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript gives explicit seasonal anchors. Those anchors outweigh the earlier 6-8 week estimate and make the March epilogue a multi-month jump, not a brief coda.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet delays Damian's full confirmation of Anya's identity to Chapter 13, but the gala scene resolves it in Chapter 12 when Erich says her full name and Damian's thought locks on 'She's Anya Forger.' Chapter 13 then describes the photo as reinforcing a conclusion already reached.
conflicts.canonical_value: Damian identifies the cultural attache as Anya Forger in chapter_12; chapter_13's class photo only confirms and revisits what he already knows.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 12 supplies the spoken name and the completed recognition. Chapter 13 and Damian's Chapter 16 confession both explicitly point back to the gala as the decisive moment.

conflicts.description: The prior sheet gives the Dominic Compound a precise 'roughly an hour's drive from central Berlint,' but the manuscript instead places it forty minutes north of Berlint proper with a remaining two-kilometer approach on foot.
conflicts.canonical_value: Dominic Compound is on Berlint's northern outskirts; precise route metrics defer to outline/spatial_layout.json.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript gives a different travel profile than the old sheet, and rule 11 makes spatial_layout.json the authoritative home for detailed route timing. The continuity sheet now keeps only the broad spatial fact.
